item.title: Hilarious
item.content:encoded: Simply wonderfully hilarious and now I wonder how much more they do with this one XD. Maybe one day we'll get the new box and cereal in it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How have I not found this before now? This has instantly become one of my - no, definitely my #1 favorite SCP on the site.

+1, though I wish I could give more.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Agreed. Added to existing proposal.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: In addition to those mentioned, I think that SCP-2076 can be reverse-engineered as well. IMO, it has the benefit of already being colourful and vibrant ads and can provide many consumers fuelling the front company's growth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Incidentally if the crosslink project ends up linking some more (maybe at least three things) to SCP-2111/RED TALISMAN like this then I will add a list to that article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added to existing proposal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That said, I feel the least invasive way of adding a link to what is largely a self contained article would be a reference to the source of the memetics

RED TALISMAN from my own SCP-2111 is exactly this, a source of anomalous memetic material that the Memetics Department mines for repackaging into targeted memes just like the Bradbury Buzzard.

ETA: I just added a clause to RED TALISMAN that supports this kind of crosslink better.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert edits at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Oh man, this is one of my favorite articles on the wiki, so getting to propose this crosslink is a real treat. That said, I feel the least invasive way of adding a link to what is largely a self contained article would be a reference to the source of the memetics used on the Super Coco Pows boxes. It seems plausible that the SCPF could reverse engineer existing memetic effects, such as that observed in SCP-2061, to achieve their initial goal, before switching to memetics of their own design as they master the craft more in later decades. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

On 05/14/1953, a Foundation front company, "Standard Products" released the "Super Coco Pows" breakfast cereal, which began marketing heavily towards children. The mascot of the brand was "Bradbury Buzzard", a cartoon caricature resembling SCP-1160. This character was featured prominently on the front and back of the box, itself containing a number of subtle anomalous memetic triggers intended to plant specific pieces of information concerning the nature and appearance of SCP-1160 into the subconscious of its targets.

To this:

On 05/14/1953, a Foundation front company, "Standard Products" released the "Super Coco Pows" breakfast cereal, which began marketing heavily towards children. The mascot of the brand was "Bradbury Buzzard", a cartoon caricature resembling SCP-1160. This character was featured prominently on the front and back of the box, itself containing a number of subtle anomalous memetic triggers intended to plant specific pieces of information concerning the nature and appearance of SCP-1160 into the subconscious of its targets1.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Initial triggers were reverse engineered from existing memetic phenomena, such as SCP-2061, SCP-2076, and RED TALISMAN.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Special Containment Procedures: Put picture on delicious cereal.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i love this so much take my +1

item.title: Re: ex-giant buzzard (SCP-1160)
item.content:encoded: Because better safe than sorry? ;>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting and completely useless bit of trivia: Brad Bird was the director of The Iron Giant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yawn, it's like reversed Bugsy with breakfast cereal and I just can't bring myself to care.

Also, if containing this thing requires spreading as much knowledge about it as possible, why are there still bits of the cereal box blacked out?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I forgot about the Cocoa Puffs bird briefly before upvoting this, and now wish I could upvote it again because the real life connection (for once) actually makes this even better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did you just turn the fucking Cocoa Puffs bird into an SCP?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This makes me laugh.
In korean, buzzard is "말똥가리"([mal-tong-ga-ri]). "말똥"([mal-tong]) means horse-shit.
If 1160 was found in Korea, the cereal could find no sale maybe, containment failure! KaBoom!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome skip. Great work, Kaktus. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kaktus, you sneaky motherfucker!

Also this SCP is really really good and you should feel good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As with most comments above, my whole-hearted approval for coming up with a great set of containment procedures, as well as a fairly logical story for how they were devised.

… is able to withstand all forms of conventional firearms, or explosives below 300kt…

I read the above as implying that explosives above 300 kilotons had be attempted and were successful in damaging the beast. Then I thought through it again and decided, no, it means that no explosives above 300 kilotons were attempted because that's a lot of kilotons. Then I thought again and realized it was just unclear to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My thoughts reading the description were initially "alright, so this is a flying 682" but the twist with the cognition and the cereals was so unexpected and creative it instantly won me over. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You made Snap, Crackle and Pop into a skip.

Snap, Crackle and Pop as a skip.

You evil genius.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn man, you are on a roll. I really ought to go back and read the rest of your stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: soggies may rule

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fuckin' brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rice Krispies.
Actually, it's rice krispies.

Don't even think about what The Three Ones really are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had just assumed that it was a rather clever play off of "General Mills"…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I mean, this thing probably dies?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just awesome. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now you've got it :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this one just for having there be an SCP cereal. I can already see it being used as a throwaway line in a tale were someone in the CI yells at a subordinate “You’re eating the enemy’s merchandise!” or something along those lines. Honestly, the only down side is that I’m a little disappointed that front company wasn’t also spelling out SCP. Sure it would have been odd to have the company and the cereal spell out SCP, but the way it is now just seems out of place. The “Standard Products” comp- The “Standard Products” Company….

SPC! We’ve been infiltrated!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't that be written in the procedures?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At it's original point of discovery on sekrit volcano island.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's cuckoo for Cocoa Puffs!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Special Chocolately Pandas?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You mentioned the Tango-77 thing, and that's neat, but where is Mr. Birdie contained?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: sweet Jesus it's all so clear now…this is either coco puffs or corn flakes….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Suddenly I have this vision of somebody (AWCY maybe?) making breakfast cereal for SCP-1055…

But yeah, the picture was amazing, and the Super Coco Pows box guaranteed the upvote.

EDIT: I noticed an "Epsilon-77" in the Description, and since Epsilon is not mentioned anywhere else, I assumed it was left over from a different draft. So I changed it to "Tango-77".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This.
I commented some about my views on containment procedure on an article of yours yesterday… and this pretty much embodies all of it by accident.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like pitting this thing against 682 just to see what happens.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neat. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The survey at the end was kind of dumb, but the rest is neat. At first I thought this would be a boring anti-bugsy, but then the TV show came into play and made it a not boring anti-bugsy. Good work kiddo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Supposedly its like they turned crack into a cereal. With 10 essential vitamins and minerals. So yeah, I'd probably eat it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Let's focus on the real issue here…is that cereal any good?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks to you guys my dreams will now be haunted by a gargantuan gorilla that puts King Kong to shame and is constantly screaming about its jimmies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the cleverest containment procedure I've ever seen. Enthusiastic and immediate upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tony the Tiger has done some shit, hasn't he?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you're not shitting your pants about Captain Crunch right now, I've done something wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was your intent making people worry about the true, unspeakable form of the Trix rabbit? Because that's what I'm doing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I adore how clever and resourceful the Foundation is portrayed as here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I said in chat, this is probably my favorite thing you have written so far kaktus.

The idea of a mighty eldritch creature brought low by breakfast cereal is delightful, not to mention the use of SCP fronts.

Sweet deal. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted as soon as I saw the picture.

The remainder did not disappoint.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was the result of somebody in chat a long time ago asking what would happen if the Foundation needed to spread awareness of something in order to keep it contained. I do not remember the person who first mentioned breakfast cereal, but to you, I am grateful.

Anyway, my sincerest thanks to everybody who looked this over and had things to say about it in chat, notably vezaz, TwistedGears, Conwell, and Silber. Your thoughts were invaluable.

As always, your comments, critiques, and criticisms are always welcome :D

Edit: Oh hello djkaktus from six years ago. Funny seeing you again.

Image credit is from here:
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tree_tops_in_an_Alsatian_forest.jpg
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Turkey_Vulture_Perched_in_a_Tree_Closeup.JPG
